EDCS-00380645.text_edition: \n Vivus Caius Iulius Leonis libertus Niger sibi et Iuliae Leonis libertae Hilarae et Caio Iulio Leonis liberto Adiuco fratri\n
EDCS-00380645.translation: Caius Julius Niger, freedman of Gaius Julius Leonis, [set this up] for himself, and for Iulia Hilara, freedwoman of Gaius Julius Leonis, and for his brother Caius Julius Adiuctus, freedman of Gaius Julius Leonis.
EDCS-00380645.summary: This funerary inscription records the setting up of a monument by a freedman for himself, his wife, and his brother, all of whom were freedmen of the same patron.

EDCS-00380646.translation: To the divine Manes and the memory of Marcianus.
EDCS-00380646.summary: This is a dedication to the Manes and memory of an individual named Marcianus.

EDCS-00380649.text_edition: \n restitutio Calvi filii Caius Acidilius pater et filia M\n
EDCS-00380649.translation: Restoration by Calvus, son of Gaius Acidilius, and his daughter M[...].
EDCS-00380649.summary: This inscription records the restoration of something by a father and daughter, likely a public work or monument.

EDCS-03000609.translation: To the divine Manes of Venenia.
EDCS-03000609.summary: This is a dedication to the divine Manes of an individual named Venenia.

EDCS-03000618.translation: [To the divine Manes] and to the memory of Virilis, the very dear maker of tunics, [set up] for himself while alive.
EDCS-03000618.summary: This funerary inscription records a man, Virilis, who was a tunic-maker, setting up a monument for himself and his wife.

EDCS-03300673.summary: This is a short inscription intended to be a memorial to Avitus.

EDCS-06100403.translation: On the death of Avitus. Ha Silex, son of Avitus, [...].
EDCS-06100403.summary: This inscription begins by marking the death of Avitus and names his son, Ha Silex, likely for a funerary monument.

EDCS-06100410.text_edition: \n Cai Iun et Titi Caius Iunxs \n
EDCS-06100410.translation: Caius Iunius [set this up] for Caius Iunius and Titus.
EDCS-06100410.summary: This inscription records Caius Iunius dedicating something, possibly a tomb or monument, to Caius Iunius and Titus.

EDCS-08300453.translation: To Tranquillus, the linen merchant, thus.
EDCS-08300453.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Tranquillus, identified as a linen merchant.

EDCS-08500466.text_edition: \n ino Anicianni sic filius Mercurio Augusto votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08500466.translation: [...ino Anicianni]. Thus, his son paid a willing and deserved vow to Mercurius Augustus.
EDCS-08500466.summary: This inscription records a son fulfilling a vow to the god Mercurius Augustus, likely in memory of his father, Aniciannus.

EDCS-08500467.text_edition: \n Silvinus Connixii filius Iunoni votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08500467.translation: Silvinus, son of Connix, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Juno.
EDCS-08500467.summary: This is a votive inscription by Silvinus to Juno.

EDCS-08500468.text_edition: \n Marcus Iulius Pyrus Matu hanc aedem \n
EDCS-08500468.translation: Marcus Julius Pyrus [built?] this temple to Matu-.
EDCS-08500468.summary: This is a dedication inscription for a temple, with Marcus Julius Pyrus as the likely dedicator.

EDCS-08600498.text_edition: \n Deo Erriape sic Taurianus et Andustinus et Taurianus lapidem post annos III deposuerunt votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-08600498.translation: Taurianus, Andustinus, and Taurianus willingly and deservedly placed this stone after 3 years and paid their vow to the god Erriapus.
EDCS-08600498.summary: This is a votive inscription by Taurianus, Andustinus, and Taurianus to the god Erriapus, marking the placement of a stone.

EDCS-08600499.text_edition: \n Deo Eriappo Castinus agens pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08600499.translation: Castinus willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the god Eriappos for the health of himself and his family.
EDCS-08600499.summary: This is a votive inscription by Castinus to the god Eriappos for his and his family's health.

EDCS-08600785.text_edition: \n Iulia Paterna votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08600785.translation: Iulia Paterna willingly and deservedly paid her vow.
EDCS-08600785.summary: This is a votive inscription by Iulia Paterna.

EDCS-08600786.text_edition: \n @(obito) Birrioni viva -risila B uxsor sic et O salve\n
EDCS-08600786.translation: Birrio having died, his wife B...risila erected this, and greetings.
EDCS-08600786.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating the deceased Birrio and erected by his wife.

EDCS-08600787.text_edition: \n Ilurgorri Severus Semperri filius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08600787.translation: Ilurgorri Severus, son of Semperri, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-08600787.summary: This is a votive inscription by Ilurgorri Severus.

EDCS-08600790.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Amandus Andosteni filius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08600790.translation: Amandus, son of Andosteni, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-08600790.summary: This is a votive inscription by Amandus to Jupiter.

EDCS-08600793.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or funerary text, mentioning Aelius C. Diphilus.

EDCS-09001529.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly recording a legal or administrative act by someone named Vergobretos.

EDCS-09200354.text_edition: \n Publi Fabi Pupi harpagion ad lippitudinem Publi Fabi Pupi mixtum militare Publi Fabi Pupi dialepidos ad diathesis sic Publi Fabi Pupi diasmyrnes lene\n
EDCS-09200354.translation: The mixture of Publius Fabius Pupi for sore eyes, the eye salve of Publius Fabius Pupi, the ointment of Publius Fabius Pupi, gently.
EDCS-09200354.summary: This is a label for a medicinal preparation, likely for eye ailments, belonging to Publius Fabius Pupi.

EDCS-09201248.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vareniae Montani filiae Montanillae Quintus Solonius Philippus uxsori sic rarissimae nnorum XXII\n
EDCS-09201248.translation: To the divine spirits of Varenia Montanilla, daughter of Montanus. Quintus Solonius Philippus to his most rare wife, aged 22 years.
EDCS-09201248.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Varenia Montanilla, erected by her husband Quintus Solonius Philippus.

EDCS-09401473.translation: To the divine spirits of the Emperors and to Minerva.
EDCS-09401473.summary: This is a dedication to the divine spirits of the emperors and the goddess Minerva.

EDCS-09800582.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus, consul, father of the fatherland, and to Marcus Julius Philippus, most noble Caesar, prince of youth, and to Otacilia Severa Augusta, mother of our Augustus and of the camps. The conventus of the Convenae (city) [is responsible for this] 1000 paces.
EDCS-09800582.summary: This is a dedication honouring Emperor Philip the Arab, his son, and his wife, erected by the conventus of the Convenae, likely marking a distance or road.

EDCS-09800583.text_edition: \n Domino nostri Marco Aurelio Carino nobillissimo Caesari principi [iuventutis ------\n
EDCS-09800583.translation: To our Lord Marcus Aurelius Carinus, most noble Caesar, prince [of youth...].
EDCS-09800583.summary: This is a dedication to Carinus, who held the title of Caesar, with the beginning of the inscription lost.

EDCS-10400178.translation: Chrysippus gladly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-10400178.summary: This is a votive inscription by Chrysippus, indicating he fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-10400316.translation: To the god Baigorisos. Geminus, son of Edunnis, gladly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-10400316.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Baigorisos, erected by Geminus.

EDCS-10400537.translation: To the mountains of Ageion. Metellinia, daughter of Quintus, gladly fulfilled her vow as deserved.
EDCS-10400537.summary: This is a votive inscription to the mountains of Ageion, erected by Metellinia.

EDCS-10400538.translation: To the god Ageion. The villagers of the ironworkers, from [their] vow.
EDCS-10400538.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Ageion, dedicated by the pagani ferrarienses (villagers of the ironworkers).

EDCS-10400539.translation: To the god Ageion. Paulinus of Taurini gladly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-10400539.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Ageion, erected by Paulinus of Taurini.

EDCS-10400540.translation: To Ageion the god, Labusius paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-10400540.summary: A votive dedication to the god Ageion by Labusius.

EDCS-10400541.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Valeria, daughter of Lucius Vara, paid her vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-10400541.summary: A votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Valeria.

EDCS-10400542.text_edition: \n Deo Stoloco Decimus Cilius Iustinus votum solvet sic levens sic mereta sic\n
EDCS-10400542.translation: To the god Stolocus, Decimus Cilius Iustinus paid his vow, as he was willing and deserving.
EDCS-10400542.summary: A votive dedication to the god Stolocus by Decimus Cilius Iustinus.

EDCS-10400543.translation: Secundus Sembedonis, on behalf of himself and of the villagers of Aquensium, placed this sacred offering to the numen of Augustus.
EDCS-10400543.summary: A sacred offering to the numen of Augustus by Secundus Sembedonis on behalf of the villagers of Aquensium and himself.

EDCS-10400545.translation: To the Nymphs, for his own health, Severius Seranus paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-10400545.summary: A votive dedication to the Nymphs for health by Severius Seranus.

EDCS-10400546.translation: To Mars the Unconquered, Gaius Minicius Potitus paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-10400546.summary: A votive dedication to Mars Invictus by Gaius Minicius Potitus.

EDCS-10400547.translation: To the August Nymphs, Lucius Victorius Censorinus, son of Gaius, and Bihossius Amianti, and Pompeia, daughter of [---], and Verinia Vera paid their vows willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-10400547.summary: A joint votive dedication to the August Nymphs by Lucius Victorius Censorinus, Bihossius Amianti, Pompeia, and Verinia Vera.

EDCS-10400548.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for T(itus) Porcius Optatus.
EDCS-10400548.summary: A tomb inscription for T. Porcius Optatus dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-10400549.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Caius Valerius Valerianus Sanctus, a most distinguished man, quaestor of the province of Baetica, his tutor, the most distinguished boy Iulius Sanctus, his son, arranged for this to be set up.
EDCS-10400549.summary: A tomb inscription for C. Valerius Valerianus Sanctus, a former quaestor of Baetica, arranged by his son and tutor Iulius Sanctus.

EDCS-10400550.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Primulus, son of Primus, for himself and his wife and his most dutiful son Verecundus.
EDCS-10400550.summary: A tomb inscription for Primulus, his wife, and his son Verecundus.

EDCS-10400561.translation: Sacred to Herauscorritse by Gaius Valerius Valerianus.
EDCS-10400561.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a deity named Herauscorritse by Gaius Valerius Valerianus.

EDCS-10400562.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Silvanius Silvinus willingly pays his vow as is deserved.
EDCS-10400562.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Silvanius Silvinus.

EDCS-10400563.translation: To the Most Holy Tutela, Chrysanthus [dedicated this].
EDCS-10400563.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Tutela by Chrysanthus.

EDCS-10400564.translation: Verus, who served as flamen, duumvir, quaestor, and master of the district, having fulfilled his duty as legate to Augustus, obtained permission for the nine peoples to join the Gauls. Returned to the city, he dedicates this altar to the genius of the district.
EDCS-10400564.summary: This inscription records Verus dedicating an altar to the genius of the district after holding several civic and military offices.

EDCS-10400566.translation: Aemilius Placidus of Pompaelo, aged 23, lies here.
EDCS-10400566.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Aemilius Placidus, who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-10400567.translation: Caius Antonius Blandus and Caius Antonius Valentinus, grandsons and heirs of Antonia Secundina, and Lucius Cornelius Valentinus, their adoptive father, had this erected in accordance with the testament of Antonia Secundina, mother of Caius Antonius Blandus, and of the same Secundina.
EDCS-10400567.summary: This funerary monument was erected by the heirs and adoptive father of Antonia Secundina, in accordance with her will.

EDCS-10400569.translation: To Valeria Prisca, Sextus Valerius [dedicated this].
EDCS-10400569.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or memorial for Valeria Prisca by Sextus Valerius.

EDCS-10400572.translation: To the god Mars, Lucius Attius Sabinianus [dedicated this].
EDCS-10400572.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mars by Lucius Attius Sabinianus.

EDCS-10400573.translation: To Mars the Lord, Licinius Myrtillus willingly pays his vow as is deserved.
EDCS-10400573.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Mars Dominus by Licinius Myrtillus.

EDCS-10400574.translation: Berullius, slave of Tiberius Claudius Sabinianus, for himself and his family, willingly pays his vow to Mars Lilhunno as is deserved.
EDCS-10400574.summary: This votive inscription to Mars Lilhunno was made by Berullius, a slave, for himself and his family.

EDCS-10400575.translation: As a vow to Mars Lehunno for his own health and that of his family, Tiberius Claudius Faustinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10400575.summary: An altar dedicated to Mars Lehunno by Tiberius Claudius Faustinus for his family's health.

EDCS-10400576.translation: To Mars Lehunno, Tiberius Claudius Sotericus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his son Domesticus.
EDCS-10400576.summary: An altar dedicated to Mars Lehunno by Tiberius Claudius Sotericus for his son.

EDCS-10400578.translation: For the health of Lucius Attius Secundus, Lucius Attius Vitalis willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10400578.summary: An altar dedicated to an unknown deity by Lucius Attius Vitalis for the health of Lucius Attius Secundus.

EDCS-10400581.summary: An incomplete inscription possibly dedicated to Quirinus.

EDCS-10400584.summary: A fragment of an inscription with illegible text.

EDCS-10400585.translation: To the god Apollo, Marcianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10400585.summary: An altar dedicated to Apollo by Marcianus.

EDCS-10400586.translation: To Hercules Tolliandosso the Invincible, Primigenius, son of Sembius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10400586.summary: An altar dedicated to Hercules Tolliandosso the Invincible by Primigenius.

EDCS-10400587.translation: Sacred to Minerva, Marcus Attius Sabinianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10400587.summary: An altar dedicated to Minerva by Marcus Attius Sabinianus.

EDCS-10400588.translation: To Minerva [---]IE[---] V[---] son(?) willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10400588.summary: A fragmentary inscription dedicated to Minerva.

EDCS-10400591.translation: To the goddess Tutela, Nexius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10400591.summary: An altar dedicated to the goddess Tutela by Nexius.

EDCS-10400592.translation: To the tutelary deity of this place, Titulinus willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-10400592.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titulinus to a local tutelary deity, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10400593.translation: Ingenua willingly and deservedly paid her vow to the Winds.
EDCS-10400593.summary: This is a votive inscription by Ingenua to the Winds, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10400594.translation: To the Divine Shades and to the memory of Domitia Charitina, Aurelius Tertinus, centurion of the 30th Legion, for his most deserving wife, who deserved so well of me.
EDCS-10400594.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Domitia Charitina, dedicated by her husband Aurelius Tertinus, a centurion, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10400595.translation: [...]uleius, while alive, a soldier of the Sixth Legion, a veteran, and for Silabina, daughter of Bortossus, and for his own family.
EDCS-10400595.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a veteran of the Sixth Legion and his family, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10400596.translation: To Gaius Afranus Graphicus, freedman of Clarus, scribe, instructor, and player of the game of latrunculi, curator of Roman citizens, and to Tertulla, his wife, according to his will.
EDCS-10400596.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Afranus Graphicus, a freedman who held various civic and professional roles, and his wife Tertulla, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10400597.summary: This is likely a funerary or dedicatory inscription mentioning Gaius Antistius Severus, who held the priestly office of flamen, probably from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10400601.translation: While alive, Albinus, freedman of Silvinus, for himself and for Cambuxa, a freedwoman.
EDCS-10400601.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Albinus, a freedman, for himself and his freedwoman partner, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10400603.translation: To the Divine Shades of Gaius Antistius Arullianus.
EDCS-10400603.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Antistius Arullianus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10400607.translation: To the Divine Shades of Balexeia, daughter of Censorinus, mother, [dedicated by?] Gaius? Julius Ce[---].
EDCS-10400607.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Balexeia, daughter of Censorinus and a mother, with a partially preserved dedication, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10400608.translation: While alive, Belexeia, daughter of Dunai, for herself and for Macro, son of Bersegius, her husband, and for Sabina, daughter of Macro. This monument shall not follow an heir.
EDCS-10400608.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Belexeia for herself, her husband Macro, and their daughter Sabina, stipulating that the burial plot is not inheritable, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10400609.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Secundus, to his wife Belissima.
EDCS-10400609.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Secundus to his deceased wife Belissima.

EDCS-10400611.summary: This inscription is likely a dedication from Toutaronia to her son Dunaio, possibly on a funerary monument.

EDCS-10400613.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Faustae, daughter of Orcuarius.
EDCS-10400613.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Faustae, daughter of Orcuarius.

EDCS-10400617.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary dedication from Aelia Pancarpe to her husband.

EDCS-10400618.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely commemorating Lucius Julius Flore[--].

EDCS-10400619.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Lucius Julius Helicon, her most cherished husband. Iulia Onesime [dedicates this].
EDCS-10400619.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Iulia Onesime to her beloved husband, Lucius Julius Helicon.

EDCS-10400620.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Lucius Julius Onesicrates, her most dutiful son. Iulia Onesime [dedicates this].
EDCS-10400620.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Iulia Onesime to her dutiful son, Lucius Julius Onesicrates.

EDCS-10400621.translation: To Lucius Julius Pal[--], freedman of Lucius, and to Gaius Iulius F[--] and Iulia [---] of the Iulii.
EDCS-10400621.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication potentially honoring multiple individuals from the Iulius family.

EDCS-10400623.translation: To Titullus Seranus, his son, and to Tutiluxsa Sole[--], daughter of Mutius, wife of the son.
EDCS-10400623.summary: This inscription seems to record a dedication to Titullus Seranus and his wife, possibly as a family monument.

EDCS-10400626.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Macria Prisca, his daughter, had this made for Macrius Serenus, her father.
EDCS-10400626.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Macria Prisca to her father, Macrius Serenus.

EDCS-10400627.text_edition: \n Vivus Martial<i=L>s Quadrat<i=L> filius sib<i=L> et @(obitae) Maxumillae Iul<i=L>i filiae uxori et natis suis \n
EDCS-10400627.translation: Martialis Quadratus, while alive, for himself, for his daughter Maxumilla Iulia, his wife, and for his children.
EDCS-10400627.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Martialis Quadratus, who set it up for himself, his wife, and children.

EDCS-10400628.translation: In good health, Matico, son of Ocellio, for himself and his family.
EDCS-10400628.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Matico, son of Ocellio, set up for himself and his family while alive.

EDCS-10400630.translation: To Gaius Octavius Faustus, son of Ahoissus, according to the will, by the authority of Saleduna Illa, his wife, daughter of Illa.
EDCS-10400630.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Octavius Faustus, established by his wife according to the terms of his will.

EDCS-10400631.translation: To the living Oiso, daughter of Oll[---], Caius Iulius Coera[nus] made this.
EDCS-10400631.summary: This funerary inscription records that Caius Iulius Coera[nus] made the monument for the living Oiso, daughter of Oll[---].

EDCS-10400632.translation: Osa, daughter of Herr[i], and Taros, son of Herr[i].
EDCS-10400632.summary: This fragmented inscription likely records the names of Osa and Taros, possibly siblings, both children of Herr[i].

EDCS-10400635.translation: [---] for the daughter of Ingenui[us?] and for Rusticus, son of [---]di.
EDCS-10400635.summary: This fragmented inscription records a dedication to an unnamed daughter of Ingenui[us?] and Rusticus, son of [---]di.

EDCS-10400636.translation: In good health, Severa, daughter of Tautinnus, for herself and her family.
EDCS-10400636.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Severa, daughter of Tautinnus, set up for herself and her family while alive.

EDCS-10400638.translation: In good health, Taurinus, son of Deri, for himself and Orguarra, daughter of Taurus, his wife, and for his sons Sambus and Tauricus, and his heirs. Let them have the right to enter up to my monument, 10 feet wide. This monument of ours shall not follow the heir, nor the heir of the heir.
EDCS-10400638.summary: This funerary inscription is set up by Taurinus, son of Deri, for himself, his wife, his sons, and his heirs, specifying burial rights and restrictions.

EDCS-10400640.translation: To Torsteginnus, son of Urupatis, and to Sabina, daughter of Primus, his wife, and to Verecunda, his daughter, and their family, according to the will.
EDCS-10400640.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication to Torsteginnus, his wife Sabina, and daughter Verecunda, established according to a will.

EDCS-10400641.translation: How sweet you were, how kind, who while alive lay in my bosom, always aware of sleep and bed. Oh, it was done badly, Myia, that you perished. If anyone approached, you would bark. Rival of your mistress, licentious one. Oh, it was done badly, Myia, that you perished. The tomb now holds you deep, unaware. You can neither be fierce nor jump, nor shine for me with sweet bites.
EDCS-10400641.summary: This is an epitaph for a beloved dog named Myia, mourning her death and recalling her affectionate and protective nature.

EDCS-10400643.summary: This is a funerary dedication to a wife, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, based on the fragmentary text suggesting familial relationship and endearment.

EDCS-10400644.translation: To his sisters, and his own [relatives?]; the place of burial is not permitted.
EDCS-10400644.summary: This fragmented funerary inscription, possibly from the Roman Imperial period, records a dedication to sisters and potentially other relatives, with a note about burial rights.

EDCS-10400645.summary: This is a funerary dedication to a wife, with the date suggested by the numerical inscription 'CVII', possibly indicating a year in the Roman dating system.

EDCS-10400668.translation: Here lies Ursinicus, a servant of Christ, innocent and a foreigner, aged 8 years, 2 months, and 10 days.
EDCS-10400668.summary: This Christian epitaph records the death of a young boy named Ursinicus, specifying his age and identifying him as a 'servant of Christ', likely dating to the later Roman Empire.

EDCS-10400672.translation: To the Mother of the Gods, Pompeia Philumene, who first performed the taurobolium in Lectorae.
EDCS-10400672.summary: This dedication honors Pompeia Philumene for performing a taurobolium, a ritual sacrifice, for the Mother of the Gods, possibly in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10400673.translation: Sacred to the Great Mother. Antonia Prima performed the taurobolium with her own victims for the priests Zmynthius Proculianus and Pacius Agrippa, in the year when Pollio and Apro were consuls for the second time, on the 15th day before the Kalends of November.
EDCS-10400673.summary: This dedication records that Antonia Prima performed a taurobolium for the Great Mother, mentioning the priests and the consuls of the year 188 CE.

EDCS-10400674.translation: Sacred to the Mother of the Gods. Aurelia Oppidana performed the taurobolium with her own victims for the priests Zmynthius Proculianus and Pacius Agrippa, in the year when Pollio and Apro were consuls for the second time, on the 15th day before the Kalends of November.
EDCS-10400674.summary: This dedication records that Aurelia Oppidana performed a taurobolium for the Mother of the Gods, mentioning the priests and the consuls of the year 188 CE.

EDCS-10400675.translation: To the Mother of the Gods. Julia Valentina and Hygia Silana performed the taurobolium on the 15th day before the Kalends of November, in the year when Pollio and Apro were consuls, with Zmynthius Proculianus as priest.
EDCS-10400675.summary: This dedication records that Julia Valentina and Hygia Silana performed a taurobolium for the Mother of the Gods, mentioning the consuls and priest, likely in 188 CE.

EDCS-10400676.translation: Sacred to the Great Mother. Aelia Nice performed the taurobolium with her own victims, with Zmynthius Proculianus as priest.
EDCS-10400676.summary: This dedication records that Aelia Nice performed a taurobolium for the Great Mother, mentioning the priest Zmynthius Proculianus, likely from the late 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10400677.translation: Sacred to the Great Mother. Marciana, daughter of Marcianus, performed the taurobolium with her own victims, with Zmynthius Proculianus the freedman as priest.
EDCS-10400677.summary: This dedication records that Marciana performed a taurobolium for the Great Mother, mentioning her father and the priest Zmynthius Proculianus, likely from the late 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10400678.translation: Sacred to the Mother of the Gods. Valeria Gemina received the taurobolium on the ninth day before the Kalends of April, when Traianus Nundinius was priest, our lord Gordian and Aviola being consuls.
EDCS-10400678.summary: This inscription records a woman, Valeria Gemina, receiving the taurobolium ritual under the consulship of Gordian and Aviola, likely in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10400679.translation: Sacred to the Mother of the Gods. For the health of the Emperor Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus and Sabinia Tranquillina Augusta and all the divine house, and for the welfare of the community of Lactoratium, the council of the Lactoratium performed the taurobolium, on the sixth day before the Ides of December, when our lord Gordian was consul for the second time and Pompeianus was consul, under the priesthood of Traianus Nundinius, with Marcus Erotio Festus and Marcus Carinius Caro presiding.
EDCS-10400679.summary: This inscription documents a collective taurobolium ritual performed by the council of Lactoratium for the well-being of the emperor and the community, during the consulship of Gordian II and Pompeianus.

EDCS-10400680.translation: Sacred to the Mother of the Gods. Gaius Julius Secundus received the taurobolium with his own hosts on the sixth day before the Ides of December, when our lord Gordian II and Pompeianus were consuls, under the priesthood of Traianus Nundinius.
EDCS-10400680.summary: This inscription records Gaius Julius Secundus receiving the taurobolium ritual, performed with his 'hosts', during the consulship of Gordian II and Pompeianus.

EDCS-10400681.translation: Sacred to the Mother of the Gods. Iulia Clementiana received the taurobolium with her own hosts on the sixth day before the Ides of December, when our lord Gordian II and Pompeianus were consuls, under the priesthood of Traianus Nundinius.
EDCS-10400681.summary: This inscription records Iulia Clementiana receiving the taurobolium ritual, performed with her 'hosts', during the consulship of Gordian II and Pompeianus.

EDCS-10400682.translation: Sacred to the Mother of the Gods. Iulia Nice received the taurobolium with her own hosts on the sixth day before the Ides of December, when our lord Gordian II and Pompeianus were consuls, under the priesthood of Traianus Nundinius.
EDCS-10400682.summary: This inscription records Iulia Nice receiving the taurobolium ritual, performed with her 'hosts', during the consulship of Gordian II and Pompeianus.

EDCS-10400683.translation: Sacred to the Mother of the Gods. Iunia Domitia received the taurobolium with her own hosts on the sixth day before the Ides of December, when our lord Gordian II and Pompeianus were consuls, under the priesthood of Traianus Nundinius.
EDCS-10400683.summary: This inscription records Iunia Domitia receiving the taurobolium ritual, performed with her 'hosts', during the consulship of Gordian II and Pompeianus.

EDCS-10400684.translation: Sacred to the Mother of the Gods. Pompeia Flora received the taurobolium with her own hosts on the sixth day before the Ides of December, when our lord Gordian II and Pompeianus were consuls, under the priesthood of Traianus Nundinius.
EDCS-10400684.summary: This inscription records Pompeia Flora receiving the taurobolium ritual, performed with her 'hosts', during the consulship of Gordian II and Pompeianus.

EDCS-10400685.translation: Sacred to the Mother of the Gods. Servilia Modesta received the taurobolium with her own hosts on the sixth day before the Ides of December, when our lord Gordian II and Pompeianus were consuls, under the priesthood of Traianus Nundinius.
EDCS-10400685.summary: This inscription records Servilia Modesta receiving the taurobolium ritual, performed with her 'hosts', during the consulship of Gordian II and Pompeianus.

EDCS-10400686.translation: Sacred to the Mother of the Gods. Valeria Gemina received the taurobolium with her own hosts on the sixth day before the Ides of December, when our lord Gordian II and Pompeianus were consuls, under the priesthood of Traianus Nundinius.
EDCS-10400686.summary: This inscription records Valeria Gemina receiving the taurobolium ritual, performed with her 'hosts', during the consulship of Gordian II and Pompeianus.

EDCS-10400687.translation: Sacred to the Mother of the Gods. Verinia Severa received the taurobolium with her own hosts on the sixth day before the Ides of December, when our lord Gordian II and Pompeianus were consuls, under the priesthood of Traianus Nundinius.
EDCS-10400687.summary: This inscription records Verinia Severa receiving the taurobolium ritual, performed with her 'hosts', during the consulship of Gordian II and Pompeianus.

EDCS-10400688.translation: For the health and safety of the divine house, the public of Lactoratenses made the tauropolium.
EDCS-10400688.summary: This inscription records a public construction project, a tauropolium, by the community of Lactoratenses, likely for imperial religious cult purposes.

EDCS-10400689.translation: Sacred. Aprilis Repentinus' son and Saturina Taurini's daughter received the tauropolium for the Great Mother, with their own sacrifices, while Lucius Accius Remus was priest.
EDCS-10400689.summary: This inscription records the reception of a tauropolium by two individuals for the Great Mother, indicating a religious dedication during the priesthood of Lucius Accius Remus.

EDCS-10400690.translation: Severus, son of Iullus, dedicated the strength of the bull, which he had made especially for the tauropolium, publicly.
EDCS-10400690.summary: This inscription records a private dedication by Severus of a representation of bull strength associated with a public tauropolium.

EDCS-10400691.translation: Sacred. Severa, Quartus' daughter, made the tauropolium for the Great Mother, with her own sacrifices.
EDCS-10400691.summary: This inscription records a private dedication of a tauropolium to the Great Mother by Severa, with her own sacrifices.

EDCS-10400692.translation: Valentina, Valentis' daughter, made the tauropolium for the Great Mother with her own sacrifices, and Valeria her daughter.
EDCS-10400692.summary: This inscription records a private dedication of a tauropolium to the Great Mother by Valentina and her daughter Valeria, including the offering of sacrifices.

EDCS-10400693.translation: Viator, son of Sabinus, dedicated the strength of the bull, which he had made especially for the tauropolium, publicly.
EDCS-10400693.summary: This inscription records a private dedication by Viator of a representation of bull strength associated with a public tauropolium.

EDCS-10400694.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the divine Antoninus, brother of the divine Verus Parthicus Maximus, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Germanicus Sarmaticus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 30th time, imperator for the 8th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, from the people of Lactoratenses.
EDCS-10400694.summary: This is a formal dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius, listing his titles and honors, erected by the people of Lactoratenses, likely during his reign.

EDCS-10400695.summary: This is a short dedication to the deified Empress Faustina.

EDCS-10400712.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to the Augusta Fortune, Titus Iulius Fidelis [dedicates this].
EDCS-10400712.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter and Fortuna Augusta by Titus Iulius Fidelis.

EDCS-10400713.translation: To the Unconquered God, Sextus Vervicius Eutyches, a cloth merchant and citizen of Trier, [dedicates this].
EDCS-10400713.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Unconquered God by Sextus Vervicius Eutyches, a cloth merchant and citizen of Trier.

EDCS-10400714.translation: For his own safety, [---] nimius gave this gift to Apollo, fulfilling a vow, perhaps with Dama Irenaeus, the father.
EDCS-10400714.summary: This is a dedication to Apollo, likely on a monument, for the well-being of the dedicator, possibly including Dama Irenaeus.

EDCS-10400715.translation: The Elusates set up [this monument] to our lord, son of the divine Antoninus Magnus, grandson of the divine Severus, Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, father of the country, consul, proconsul.
EDCS-10400715.summary: This is a public dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander, set up by the people of Elusa, dating to his reign (222-235 CE).

EDCS-10400722.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Aedunnia Hermione, a most rare woman, an incomparable wife, erected by her husband Aemilius Fronto and her son Pompeius Lepidus.
EDCS-10400722.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Aedunnia Hermione, set up by her husband and son, likely on a tomb.

EDCS-10400723.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. He made these [tombs/monuments] while alive for his wife, daughter of Albenus Ambillus and daughter of Laevina Aucasia.
EDCS-10400723.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a man for himself and his wife, indicating the construction of their tomb during their lifetime.

EDCS-10400724.summary: This is a dedication or epitaph for Cerdon, a freedman of Procula.

EDCS-10400725.translation: [---]. Gaius Julius Tarros, son of Talsconis, and Iulia Accaten, daughter of Condatus, his wife, Gaius Julius Paullus, son, Titus Julius Sabinus, son.
EDCS-10400725.summary: This inscription lists several family members, likely associated with a tomb or a property, with father-son and husband-wife relationships indicated.

EDCS-10400728.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely an epitaph, identifying Vertycius Eutyches as the father.

EDCS-10400737.translation: To the Iunones, for Iulia and Sextilia [---].
EDCS-10400737.summary: This is a dedication to the Iunones, protective female deities, for two women named Iulia and Sextilia.

EDCS-10400738.translation: Escingus, son of Bassus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for his sons and household slaves.
EDCS-10400738.summary: This is a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Escingus for the well-being of his sons and slaves.

EDCS-10400739.translation: Martialis donated a small altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus with the temple and doors, at his own expense.
EDCS-10400739.summary: This is a dedication by Martialis of a small altar, including the temple and doors, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus at his own expense.

EDCS-10400741.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Livia Divogena ordered this to be set up. The heir took care of it with 150 denarii.
EDCS-10400741.summary: This funerary inscription records that Livia Divogena ordered a monument to be set up, which was completed by her heir, funded by 150 denarii.

EDCS-10400743.translation: For Natalicii Viribus. By Valeria Iullina and Iulia Sancta.
EDCS-10400743.summary: This is a dedication by Valeria Iullina and Iulia Sancta, possibly for a tomb or a religious purpose.

EDCS-10400744.translation: Sacred to Mercury for the health of Graecinus. Autumnalis, slave of Graecinus, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10400744.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Mercury made by Autumnalis, a slave of Graecinus, for his master's well-being.

EDCS-10400745.translation: Sacred to Mercury. Pompeia Thelegusa, mother of the sacred rites, willingly fulfilled her vow. Attalus.
EDCS-10400745.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Mercury made by Pompeia Thelegusa, identified as a 'mater sacrorum', with a separate mention of Attalus.

EDCS-10400746.translation: To Mercury Viducus. The citizens of Lemovices willingly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-10400746.summary: This is a votive inscription to Mercury Viducus made by the citizens of Lemovices.

EDCS-10400747.translation: To Mercury Augustus. Visucio Iulius Montanus, freedman of Titus Iulius Secundus Faustus, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10400747.summary: This votive dedication to Mercury Augustus was made by Visucio Iulius Montanus, a freedman of Titus Iulius Secundus Faustus.

EDCS-10400748.summary: This is a simple votive dedication by a person named Geminus.

EDCS-10400749.translation: To the divine will of Augustus. [---] to the goddess Divixtos. Gemellus, son of [---], father, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10400749.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the divine will of Augustus and the goddess Divixtos, made by Gemellus, identified as a father.

EDCS-10400752.translation: To Sirona. Adbucietus, son of Tocetus, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10400752.summary: This votive inscription is dedicated to the goddess Sirona by Adbucietus, son of Tocetus.

EDCS-10400756.translation: To Sirona(?). Marcus Sulpicius Primulus, a seviral from Turiassone, took care to have this made at his own expense. Sulpicius Sacuro, son. Sulpicia Censorina, daughter. Sulpicia Phoebe, freedwoman.
EDCS-10400756.summary: This inscription records the construction of a monument to Sirona, initiated by Marcus Sulpicius Primulus, a local official, and mentions his family members and freedwoman.

EDCS-10400758.translation: Gaius Julius Severus set this up in fulfillment of a vow, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-10400758.summary: A votive dedication by Gaius Julius Severus, likely a private individual.

EDCS-10400763.translation: [His] freedman and also heir, who was an Augustalis, a decurial officer, a civic lictor, and died at the age of 70, saw to its erection.
EDCS-10400763.summary: A funerary monument set up by the freedman and heir of the deceased, who held several civic and Augustalis positions.

EDCS-10400764.translation: Antonia Severiane, his daughter and heir, [dedicated this to her father], a soldier of the II Parthica legion, who lived about 37 years and served [3] years.
EDCS-10400764.summary: A funerary monument for a soldier of the II Parthica legion, dedicated by his daughter and heir.

EDCS-10400765.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelius Summus, a soldier of the Hemes(e)norum, a citizen of Moesia(?), who lived 33 years, made this for himself at his own expense; his fellow soldiers saw to its erection.
EDCS-10400765.summary: A self-funded funerary monument for Aurelius Summus, a soldier of the Hemes(e)norum, with his fellow soldiers completing the erection.

EDCS-10400767.translation: Gaius Julius Secundus, the praetor, bequeathed 2,000,000 sesterces for the aqueducts in his will.
EDCS-10400767.summary: A bequest by Gaius Julius Secundus, a praetor, for the funding of aqueducts, recorded in his will.

EDCS-10400771.summary: Fragment of an inscription mentioning Gaius Julius Aug(ustalis) and a collapsed structure, likely public.

EDCS-10400772.translation: To the departed spirits. Julia Avita, flaminica, who died at the age of 68. Gaius Julius Avitus set this up for his mother.
EDCS-10400772.summary: A funerary monument for Julia Avita, a flaminica, set up by her son Gaius Julius Avitus.

EDCS-10400774.translation: [...] quaestor, magister pag[i ...] added the area [...]
EDCS-10400774.summary: A fragment of an inscription referring to a quaestor and magister pagi adding an area, likely for public use.

EDCS-10400777.translation: Of Lucius Ammius Silvinus, citizen of Amiens, who died at the age of 45. Silvina, daughter of Lepidus, a relative, and the guardian, daughter of Palme Aelius, [saw to it].
EDCS-10400777.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Ammius Silvinus, a citizen of Amiens, with contributions from relatives and a guardian.

EDCS-10400779.translation: To the departed spirits and the memory of Valeria Victorina, a citizen of Aquae, who died at the age of 60. Her sons saw to its erection and dedicated it under the axe.
EDCS-10400779.summary: A funerary monument for Valeria Victorina, a citizen of Aquae, erected and dedicated by her sons.

EDCS-10400781.translation: To the spirits of the departed, in memory of Vestinus Onate, a citizen of the Belgae, who died at the age of thirty-four.
EDCS-10400781.summary: A funerary monument for Vestinus Onate, a Belgae citizen who died at 34.

EDCS-10400782.translation: To Lucius Antonius Statutus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, from Bilbilis, by his freedman Ocellio.
EDCS-10400782.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a freedman to his former master, Lucius Antonius Statutus of Bilbilis.

EDCS-10400783.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Castricia of the Bituriges Vivisca, who died at the age of five; Castrensis, her father, arranged for this to be set up.
EDCS-10400783.summary: A funerary monument set up by a father for his daughter Castricia, a child of the Bituriges Vivisca.

EDCS-10400784.translation: Julius Lupus, a citizen of the Bituriges Urbici, who died at the age of thirty-five; his son arranged for this to be set up.
EDCS-10400784.summary: A funerary monument set up by a son for his deceased father, Julius Lupus, a Bituriges Urbicus.

EDCS-10400785.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Saturninus Privatus, a citizen of the Boii, who died at the age of thirty-seven; his wife, Julia Epomima, donated this plot.
EDCS-10400785.summary: A funerary monument where a wife dedicated a burial plot for her husband Saturninus Privatus, a Boii citizen.

EDCS-10400786.translation: To the spirits of the departed and in memory of Donata, who died at the age of fifty-one; her husband Reginianus, a citizen of Coriosolis, arranged for this to be set up.
EDCS-10400786.summary: A funerary monument set up by a husband for his deceased wife Donata, a citizen of Coriosolis.

EDCS-10400787.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Flavius Pollianus Eburio, a physician, who died at the age of thirty-six; his wife Valeria arranged for this to be set up.
EDCS-10400787.summary: A funerary monument set up by a wife for her deceased husband Flavius Pollianus Eburio, a physician.

EDCS-10400788.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Junius Regulus, who died at the age of twenty-six, by birth a German; his dearest wife Julia Thais placed this.
EDCS-10400788.summary: A funerary monument placed by a wife for her deceased husband Junius Regulus, a German.

EDCS-10400789.translation: To the spirits of the departed and in memory of Heuplus, who died at the age of thirty, by birth a Greek; P. G., a Greek citizen(?).
EDCS-10400789.summary: A funerary monument for Heuplus, a Greek who died at 30, possibly set up by another Greek.

EDCS-10400790.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Marcus Publicius Diofantus, a Greek citizen, who died at the age of fifty-one; his wife Publicia Primitiva placed this.
EDCS-10400790.summary: A funerary monument placed by a wife for her deceased husband Marcus Publicius Diofantus, a Greek citizen.

EDCS-10400791.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Hostilius Liberalis, freedman and heir, had this made according to the will for Lucius Hostilius Saturninus the Spaniard, from Curonia.
EDCS-10400791.summary: A funerary dedication by a freedman and heir for his deceased patron, Lucius Hostilius Saturninus, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-10400792.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to the memory of Inventa, citizen of Limoges, who died at 24 years of age. Martinus, her husband, placed this.
EDCS-10400792.summary: A funerary monument set up by a husband for his wife, Inventa, a citizen of Limoges who died at 24, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-10400793.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Favor, deceased at 30 years of age, a craftsman from Mediomatrici, [his] father(?).
EDCS-10400793.summary: A funerary inscription for Favor, a craftsman of Mediomatric origin who died at 30, with a partial mention of his father, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10400794.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to the memory of Pompeia Menapia, deceased at 38 years of age. Pompeius Iunius placed this for his dearest wife, in recognition of her merits.
EDCS-10400794.summary: A funerary dedication by Pompeius Iunius for his wife, Pompeia Menapia, who died at 38, recognizing her merits, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10400795.translation: [...], by nationality a Nicomedian, deceased at 40 years of age. Valeria Secundina, his wife, had this set up.
EDCS-10400795.summary: A funerary inscription for a Nicomedian man who died at 40, set up by his wife, Valeria Secundina, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10400796.summary: A very brief funerary record for Serdus, a citizen of Paris, who died at 50, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10400799.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to the memory of Valerius Secundus, a Ruthenian, deceased at [an unspecified age]. Secundinus, his son, had this set up.
EDCS-10400799.summary: A funerary dedication by a son for his father, Valerius Secundus of the Ruteni tribe, whose age is lost, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10400800.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to the memory of Iulia Europa Saia, deceased at 25 years of age. T. P. had this set up at his own expense.
EDCS-10400800.summary: A funerary dedication by T. P. (name incomplete) for Iulia Europa Saia, who died at 25, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10400801.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Iulius Mutacus of the Sequani. Quintus Ignius Sextus, his brother.
EDCS-10400801.summary: A funerary dedication by a brother, Quintus Ignius Sextus, for Lucius Iulius Mutacus of the Sequani, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10400803.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to the memory of Domitia, citizen of Trier, deceased at 20 years of age. Leo placed this for his dearest wife. Here lies the lifeless body of Domitia, citizen of Trier, deceased on the 5th day before the Kalends of February, in the consulship of Postumus.
EDCS-10400803.summary: A detailed funerary inscription for Domitia, a citizen of Trier who died at 20, set up by her husband Leo, with a precise date of death in the consulship of Postumus (likely 58 or 55 BCE, or 14 BCE).

EDCS-10400804.translation: To the shades of the underworld. The heir took care to erect this for Lucius Solimarius Secundinus, a citizen of Trier, a merchant trading with Britain.
EDCS-10400804.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Solimarius Secundinus, a merchant from Trier, set up by his heir.

EDCS-10400805.translation: To the shades of the underworld and for the memory of Vildicus, a citizen of Trier, aged 35. Nasbinius(?) dedicated (this).
EDCS-10400805.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vildicus, a 35-year-old citizen of Trier, with Nasbinius as the dedicator.

EDCS-10400806.translation: To the shades of the underworld and for the memory of Marcus Valerius Charidemus, a citizen of Vienne, who set this up for himself and his family while alive.
EDCS-10400806.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Valerius Charidemus, a citizen of Vienne, set up by himself for his family.

EDCS-10400807.translation: To the shades of the underworld and for the memory of Valeria Iullina, wife of Marcus Valerius Charidemus, a citizen of Vienne. Marcus Valerius Charidemus set this up while alive.
EDCS-10400807.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valeria Iullina, wife of Marcus Valerius Charidemus, set up by her husband.

EDCS-10400808.translation: To the shades of the underworld and for the memory of [name illegible], a most dutiful wife, aged 35.
EDCS-10400808.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a deceased wife, aged 35, with her name illegible.

EDCS-10400809.translation: To the shades of the underworld and for the memory of Dixtus, a fuller, deceased, aged 65. Gerotius and Centurio, brothers, took care to place this.
EDCS-10400809.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Dixtus, a fuller aged 65, erected by his brothers Gerotius and Centurio.

EDCS-10400810.translation: To the shades of the underworld. [Marcus?] S() NV(?), a doctor, deceased, aged 51, [is] buried [here].
EDCS-10400810.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a doctor aged 51, with his name partially illegible.

EDCS-10400811.translation: To the shades of the underworld. Calenus, the painter.
EDCS-10400811.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Calenus, a painter.

EDCS-10400812.translation: To the shades of the underworld and for the memory of Aurelius Paedocaeus, an actor and merchant, deceased, aged 75.
EDCS-10400812.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Paedocaeus, an actor and merchant aged 75.

EDCS-10400813.text_edition: \n Marco Se Amabili sculptori Amandus frater curavit\n
EDCS-10400813.translation: Amandus, his brother, erected (this) for Marcus Se Amabilis, the sculptor.
EDCS-10400813.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Se Amabilis, a sculptor, erected by his brother Amandus.

EDCS-10400815.translation: To the departed spirits: for Galulerclus and all the ancestors, Duetil Tiblik and Eppimus Soris, the collaborators, [dedicated this] well.
EDCS-10400815.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to departed spirits and to a group of collaborators, possibly from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10400816.translation: To the departed spirit and memory of Tertiolus, deceased aged 70 years. Diaria and Iovensium gave [this] to [their] father and companion.
EDCS-10400816.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication to the memory of Tertiolus, deceased at the age of 70, by Diaria and Iovensium, identified as his father and companion.

EDCS-10400818.translation: To the departed spirits: from a father to Amabilis.
EDCS-10400818.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription indicating a father's dedication to his deceased child, Amabilis.

EDCS-10400819.translation: To the departed spirits: Amabilis's children dedicated [this].
EDCS-10400819.summary: A funerary inscription stating that the children of Amabilis dedicated this monument to him.

EDCS-10400821.translation: For Antonia, deceased aged 37 years. Her husband Strato(?) arranged for this to be set up.
EDCS-10400821.summary: A funerary inscription for Antonia, who died at 37 years old, with her husband Strato(?) arranging the monument.

EDCS-10400822.translation: To the departed spirits and memory of Antonia Lavinia. Veranius Verus, her husband, and Antonia Augusta, [dedicated this] gift to their most devoted mother.
EDCS-10400822.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by the husband and daughter (Antonia Augusta) to Antonia Lavinia, their devoted wife and mother.

EDCS-10400823.translation: Apri, son of Perpetuus, his wife Cinto, set [this] up.
EDCS-10400823.summary: This inscription records that Cinto set up a monument for her husband, Apri Perpetuus.

EDCS-10400824.translation: To the departed spirits: Sanctia Firma placed [this] for her most devoted husband, Quintus Appius Laelianus, from her own resources.
EDCS-10400824.summary: This funerary inscription records Sanctia Firma dedicating a monument from her own funds to her devoted husband, Quintus Appius Laelianus.

EDCS-10400826.translation: To the departed spirits and memory of Ateuritus.
EDCS-10400826.summary: A brief funerary inscription dedicated to the memory of Ateuritus.

EDCS-10400827.translation: To the departed spirits and memory of Gaius Atilius Romanus, deceased aged 3 years and 35 days. Romulus, his father, set [this] up for his dearest son.
EDCS-10400827.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by Romulus for his son, Gaius Atilius Romanus, who died at the age of 3 years and 35 days.

EDCS-10400829.translation: To the departed spirits. Atioxus and Craxillus erected this monument for their mother.
EDCS-10400829.summary: A funerary monument erected by two individuals for their mother.

EDCS-10400830.translation: To the memory of Atti Priscianus, deceased, aged 38 years.
EDCS-10400830.summary: A funerary inscription for Atti Priscianus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-10400831.translation: To the departed spirits and to the memory of Attia, aged 30 years.
EDCS-10400831.summary: A funerary inscription for Attia, recording her age at death.

EDCS-10400832.translation: To the departed spirits and to the memory of Attusiola, deceased, aged 50 years.
EDCS-10400832.summary: A funerary inscription for Attusiola, recording her age at death.

EDCS-10400833.translation: To the departed spirits and to the memory of Aventi...
EDCS-10400833.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription mentioning Aventi.

EDCS-10400834.translation: ... for Aveta, deceased, aged 25 years. Her mother Cintugena erected this.
EDCS-10400834.summary: A funerary inscription for Aveta, erected by her mother, recording her age at death.

EDCS-10400835.translation: To the departed spirits and to the memory of Aveteso, deceased, aged 2 years.
EDCS-10400835.summary: A funerary inscription for Aveteso, recording his age at death.

EDCS-10400836.translation: To the departed spirits. Aveta Nanti, deceased, aged 75 years. Julius Divixtamus erected this for his beloved wife.
EDCS-10400836.summary: A funerary inscription erected by a husband for his deceased wife, recording her age.

EDCS-10400838.translation: To the departed spirits. For Marcus Augustalius Martinus, deceased, aged 3[something]5 years. Augustalius Salvus, the patron, saw to its erection.
EDCS-10400838.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Augustalius Martinus, likely erected by his patron, with a partially legible age.

EDCS-10400839.translation: To the departed spirits. For Augustus Cratini Sin[...]
EDCS-10400839.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription mentioning Augustus and Cratinus.

EDCS-10400840.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to the memory of Aurilia S[---]ae, who lived 35 years. Sallustius(?) placed [this] for his excellent wife.
EDCS-10400840.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Aurilia S[---]ae after 35 years of life and was erected by her husband, Sallustius(?).

EDCS-10400844.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to the memory of Basileus, aged 51 years, as a gift from Ianur(i?).
EDCS-10400844.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Basileus, aged 51, and was set up by Ianur(i?) as a dedication.

EDCS-10400845.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cobea placed [this] for her sister Batrusconi.
EDCS-10400845.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Cobea dedicated a monument to her deceased sister, Batrusconi.

EDCS-10400846.translation: To the spirits of the departed for Belinia Sau[3]lia, daughter, deceased, aged 2[--].
EDCS-10400846.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Belinia Sau[3]lia, daughter, who died at the age of 2[--].

EDCS-10400848.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to the memory of Caelia Regina, deceased, aged 31 years. Her brother took care to place [this].
EDCS-10400848.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Caelia Regina, aged 31, and was placed by her brother.

EDCS-10400849.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Camulia placed [this] for her fathers Blastus and Ivorigus.
EDCS-10400849.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Camulia dedicated a monument to her deceased fathers, Blastus and Ivorigus.

EDCS-10400851.translation: [---] son of Camul(us?), deceased, aged [---]. [---] the freedman of Camulini placed [this] for his patron [---].
EDCS-10400851.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of the son of Camul(us?) and the dedication of a monument by a freedman to his patron.

EDCS-10400852.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Camulus, deceased, aged 20 years. O[--].
EDCS-10400852.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Camulus at the age of 20.

EDCS-10400853.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to the memory of Capelliana, deceased.
EDCS-10400853.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Capelliana.

EDCS-10400854.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to the memory of Cassius Tiberius, deceased, aged 65 years. His daughter and brother took care to place [this].
EDCS-10400854.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Cassius Tiberius, aged 65, and was placed by his daughter and brother.

EDCS-10400855.translation: To the departed spirits. Acaunus Senorucci, having died at the age of 48, placed [this]. His daughter Sentilla.
EDCS-10400855.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Acaunus Senorucci, set up by his daughter Sentilla.

EDCS-10400856.translation: Celatus(?), having died at the age of 23.
EDCS-10400856.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph, likely for a person named Celatus.

EDCS-10400857.translation: Censorinus, freedman of Tasgillus, once deceased at the age of 94, son and brothers, [perhaps] our father, placed [this]. Centurion son and brothers placed [this] for our father.
EDCS-10400857.summary: This funerary epitaph records Censorinus, a freedman and centurion, and was likely erected by his sons and brothers.

EDCS-10400858.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription, possibly a funerary epitaph or a dedication.

EDCS-10400861.translation: To the departed spirits. To Cintucena Aquitani, daughter, deceased at the age of 50.
EDCS-10400861.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cintucena Aquitani, honoring her death at 50.

EDCS-10400862.translation: To the departed spirits. To Cintugena, daughter of I[---]nis, deceased at the age of 25. Quintus Valerius Amabilis placed [this] at his own expense.
EDCS-10400862.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Cintugena, daughter of I[---]nis, and was erected by Quintus Valerius Amabilis.

EDCS-10400863.translation: [To the departed spirits]. To Cintugena, daughter of Solimarus, deceased at the age of 35. Her husband Cinto gave [this] at his own expense.
EDCS-10400863.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Cintugena, daughter of Solimarus, and was erected by her husband Cinto.

EDCS-10400864.translation: To the departed spirits. Cintugenus, son of Tesconis, deceased at the age of 2.
EDCS-10400864.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of a child named Cintugenus, son of Tesconis.

EDCS-10400865.translation: To the departed spirits. Cintucnatus, deceased at the age of 30.
EDCS-10400865.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cintucnatus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-10400867.translation: To the departed spirits. To Cintusma, daughter of Senodon, Gaius Julius Corr[---], deceased at the age of 20.
EDCS-10400867.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cintusma, daughter of Senodon, likely erected by Gaius Julius Corr[---].

EDCS-10400868.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cintusmus [died].
EDCS-10400868.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed, honoring a person named Cintusmus, with illegible details about their death.

EDCS-10400869.translation: To the spirits of the departed. His heirs placed this for Comagus, son of Cintusmus, who died at 30 years of age.
EDCS-10400869.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by heirs for Comagus, son of Cintusmus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-10400871.translation: [---]. For his wife Buccula. Claudius Bassinus [placed this].
EDCS-10400871.summary: This funerary dedication is for Buccula, placed by her husband Claudius Bassinus.

EDCS-10400872.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [---] of Titus C. Cinto[nis].
EDCS-10400872.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed, possibly for someone named Titus C. Cinto[nis].

EDCS-10400874.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Claudia Aila.
EDCS-10400874.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Claudia Aila.

EDCS-10400875.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to the memory of Claudia Contempta, who died at 15 years of age. Her father(?) took care to place this.
EDCS-10400875.summary: This funerary monument is dedicated to the memory of Claudia Contempta, who died at 15, and was likely placed by her father.

EDCS-10400876.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to [the memory] of Claudia Crispina, who died at 27 years of age. Her father took care to place this.
EDCS-10400876.summary: This funerary monument honors Claudia Crispina, who died at 27, and was placed by her father.

EDCS-10400877.translation: [---]. For Claudia Doris, who died at 46 years of age. Lucius Julius Sollemnis, her husband, took care to place this.
EDCS-10400877.summary: This funerary inscription is for Claudia Doris, who died at 46, and was placed by her husband Lucius Julius Sollemnis.

EDCS-10400878.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Claudia Materna, daughter of Adnametus, who died at 30 years of age, and for Aprilis, son of Cintuginatus, who died at 28 years of age.
EDCS-10400878.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates two individuals: Claudia Materna, daughter of Adnametus (died at 30), and Aprilis, son of Cintuginatus (died at 28).

EDCS-10400879.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Clodia Rufina, who died at 81 years of age. Clodia Secundilla, her freedwoman, placed this.
EDCS-10400879.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Clodia Rufina at 81 years of age, and was placed by her freedwoman, Clodia Secundilla.

EDCS-10400882.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to the memory of the deceased, aged 75, Conmolnicus had it put in place.
EDCS-10400882.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Conmolnicus, who died at the age of 75.

EDCS-10400884.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to the memory of Cornelia, Dic() had it put in place, deceased aged [..].
EDCS-10400884.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Cornelia, for whom Dic() arranged the monument.

EDCS-10400886.translation: [...], deceased, aged 25, Crescentinus, husband of Cresimus, had it put in place.
EDCS-10400886.summary: This is a funerary dedication for an unnamed woman, erected by her husband Crescentinus.

EDCS-10400887.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to the memory of Crispa, aged 30, ARTIN(?) OLLI, who lived by her virtue, made this.
EDCS-10400887.summary: This is a funerary monument for Crispa, made by OLLI, likely her partner, who commemorates her virtue.

EDCS-10400888.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to the memory of Cyllenus, deceased, aged 2.
EDCS-10400888.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Cyllenus, who died in infancy.

EDCS-10400890.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Aurelius Turinus, son of Dagobius and Comartiorix(?), deceased, aged 33.
EDCS-10400890.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Turinus, son of Dagobius and Comartiorix, who died at age 33.

EDCS-10400892.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Divixta, freedwoman of Paterninus, deceased, aged 21.
EDCS-10400892.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Divixta, a freedwoman of Paterninus, who died at age 21.

EDCS-10400893.translation: To Domitia Peregrina, Domitius Abascantus, her most devoted mother.
EDCS-10400893.summary: This is a dedication to Domitia Peregrina by her son Domitius Abascantus.

EDCS-10400894.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Draucus, son of Cornicus, erected [this].
EDCS-10400894.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Draucus, son of Cornicus, erected by an unnamed person.

EDCS-10400896.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Durnacus, deceased, aged 70.
EDCS-10400896.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Durnacus, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-10400897.translation: To Ebucia Materna, daughter of Marcus, and Gaius Iulius Maxumus, for 58 months, and Lucius Ebicius Atec[...]
EDCS-10400897.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary dedication for multiple individuals, likely related, with a specified duration of 58 months for one of the deceased.

EDCS-10400898.translation: To the spirits of the underworld and to memory. Ennia Teodothe, deceased, aged 41 years, was seen to by Publius Iulius Nundinus.
EDCS-10400898.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Ennia Teodothe at age 41, with Publius Iulius Nundinus acting as the curator.

EDCS-10400899.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Of Fabricia Cocceiana, aged 50 years.
EDCS-10400899.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Fabricia Cocceiana, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-10400900.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Felix, my son, deceased, aged 12 years. From Felicissimus, father.
EDCS-10400900.summary: A funerary inscription where a father, Felicissimus, commemorates his son Felix, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-10400901.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Festiva, daughter of Natalis. Natalis, father, placed [this].
EDCS-10400901.summary: A funerary monument set up by Natalis, a father, for his daughter Festiva.

EDCS-10400902.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Festiva, deceased, aged 23 years. Her husband, Taluppa, arranged for this to be placed.
EDCS-10400902.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Festiva at age 23 and states that her husband Taluppa arranged for the monument.

EDCS-10400903.translation: To the spirits of the underworld and to memory. For Flavius Victor Vitalis, son, deceased, aged 28 years and 8 months and 13 days, and for his mother Iulia Divica, deceased, aged 48 years.
EDCS-10400903.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating two individuals, Flavius Victor Vitalis (aged 28 years, 8 months, 13 days) and his mother Iulia Divica (aged 48 years).

EDCS-10400905.translation: To the spirits of the underworld and to memory. For Fulgentius, deceased, aged 2 years. Father placed [this].
EDCS-10400905.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fulgentius, who died at the young age of 2, with the inscription placed by his father.

EDCS-10400906.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a funerary dedication, mentioning the name Gallicana.

EDCS-10400907.translation: To the spirits of the underworld and to memory. For Graecinia Bl (.)nda, deceased, aged 31 years. Her husband placed [this] for his dearest wife.
EDCS-10400907.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by a husband to his wife, Graecinia Bl(.)nda, who died at the age of 31.

EDCS-10400908.translation: Heleno, having died at the age of 21, his father Noniconius Senecianus took care to place [this monument].
EDCS-10400908.summary: A funerary epitaph for Heleno, aged 21, placed by his father.

EDCS-10400909.translation: Marcus Histimennius Treptus [erected this] for himself and his family while alive, for his daughter Histimenia Rufina and his sons Faustus and Rufinus.
EDCS-10400909.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Histimennius Treptus, who set it up for himself and his family.

EDCS-10400910.translation: [To the spirits of the departed] and to the memory of Horcola Sortoletonis, who died at the age of 65, her daughter Maritalis took care to place [this monument].
EDCS-10400910.summary: A funerary epitaph for Horcola Sortoletonis, aged 65, placed by her daughter.

EDCS-10400911.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sextus Hostilius Urbanicanus lived 40 years, more or less. His kin placed [this monument].
EDCS-10400911.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextus Hostilius Urbanicanus, who lived approximately 40 years.

EDCS-10400912.translation: For Ianuaria, who died at the age of 28.
EDCS-10400912.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ianuaria, aged 28.

EDCS-10400913.translation: For Ianurio, who died at the age of 10 [?].
EDCS-10400913.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Ianurio, aged approximately 10.

EDCS-10400914.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Ianurio, a cupbearer (?).
EDCS-10400914.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for a cupbearer named Ianurio.

EDCS-10400915.translation: Ianuaria, who died at the age of 38.
EDCS-10400915.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ianuaria, aged 38.

EDCS-10400916.translation: To the memory of INCIAVVERVAVS, who died at the age of 31. S(ibi) P(osuit) [?].
EDCS-10400916.summary: A funerary epitaph for INCIAVVERVAVS, aged 31.

EDCS-10400917.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Inderca, daughter of Indercillus.

EDCS-10400920.translation: To Gaius Julius Andelipus, son of Aquitanus; Iulia Aduorix, his wife and heir, had this erected according to his will.
EDCS-10400920.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a wife for her deceased husband, Gaius Julius Andelipus, at the instruction of his will.

EDCS-10400921.translation: Iulius Aplonius and Annia Quieta, his wife, placed this.
EDCS-10400921.summary: This funerary monument was placed by Iulius Aplonius and his wife, Annia Quieta.

EDCS-10400923.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Iulius Cintu[---].
EDCS-10400923.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirit of Iulius Cintu[---].

EDCS-10400925.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for the deceased Iulius Divicus, 48 years old. Caisiccus placed this.
EDCS-10400925.summary: This is a funerary monument for Iulius Divicus, who died at the age of 48, placed by Caisiccus.

EDCS-10400926.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Gaius Julius Homullus, of the Salian tribe (?), his most devoted wife.
EDCS-10400926.summary: This funerary inscription honors Gaius Julius Homullus, with a mention of his devoted wife.

EDCS-10400927.translation: [---] for Iulius Iulianus [---] for Iulia Restituta, deceased aged 10 [---]. [---] Iulianus [---] donated the site, her husband.
EDCS-10400927.summary: This inscription records a husband, Iulianus, donating a burial plot for his deceased wife, Iulia Restituta, and possibly a son, Iulius Iulianus.

EDCS-10400928.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Iulius Polychronius Matronae, son, deceased, aged 9 years. Candidus, his freedman, had this erected.
EDCS-10400928.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Candidus, a freedman, for his young master, Iulius Polychronius Matronae.

EDCS-10400929.translation: [---] Ulpuro [---] had this made according to the will.
EDCS-10400929.summary: This inscription indicates that a monument was constructed according to a will for someone named Ulpuro.

EDCS-10400930.translation: [---]nae, his daughter, aged 5 years, deceased at the same time. Iulius Romulus erected this for his wife and daughter.
EDCS-10400930.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Iulius Romulus for his wife and daughter, who died together, one of whom was aged five.

EDCS-10400932.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Iulius Saturionius, deceased, aged 25 years, 11 months. Iulius Saturninus, father, placed this.
EDCS-10400932.summary: This funerary monument was placed by Iulius Saturninus for his son, Iulius Saturionius, who died at the age of 25 years and 11 months.

EDCS-10400933.translation: Julius Senecianus, deceased, aged 31. Ponis[3], his wife.
EDCS-10400933.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Julius Senecianus, who died at the age of 31, erected by his wife.

EDCS-10400934.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Severus, son of Sanuacus, deceased, aged 35. Aterta, his mother, saw to its placement.
EDCS-10400934.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Severus, son of Sanuacus, who died at 35, set up by his mother.

EDCS-10400935.translation: To Marcus Julius Severus, son of Gaius, of the Voltinian tribe, according to his will.
EDCS-10400935.summary: This inscription records a posthumous dedication or endowment by will for Marcus Julius Severus.

EDCS-10400936.translation: To the departed spirits. Julius Siles(?), son of Ian(uarius?), deceased, aged 51. He saw to its placement.
EDCS-10400936.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Julius Siles(?), son of Ian(uarius?), who died at 51, with the deceased himself having arranged for its erection.

EDCS-10400937.translation: To the departed spirits and to the memory of Iulia Soilla, deceased, aged 78. Iulia Albil/la placed this for her freedman.
EDCS-10400937.summary: This funerary epitaph is for Iulia Soilla, who died at 78, erected by Iulia Albil/la for her freedman.

EDCS-10400939.translation: [To the departed spirits]. Tiberi, aged 16 months. Gaius Julius Taurianus, father, and Maximilla, mother.
EDCS-10400939.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiberi, who died at 16 months, set up by his parents Gaius Julius Taurianus and Maximilla.

EDCS-10400940.translation: Lucius Julius [3], deceased, aged [3]1. Pr() father(?) saw to its placement.
EDCS-10400940.summary: This funerary epitaph records Lucius Julius [name fragment], who died at an unstated age, arranged for by his father.

EDCS-10400943.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Betudaca, daughter of Matui, aged 45.
EDCS-10400943.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Betudaca, daughter of Matui, who died at 45.

EDCS-10400945.translation: To the departed spirits and to the memory of Iulia Ingenua, deceased, aged 61. Her husband saw to its placement.
EDCS-10400945.summary: This funerary epitaph is for Iulia Ingenua, who died at 61, erected by her husband.

EDCS-10400946.summary: This inscription likely records the names and relationships of two women, possibly a mother and daughter, with partial restoration.

EDCS-10400947.translation: To Marcus Salvius Paterno(?), son of Quintus Salvius As[---].
EDCS-10400947.summary: This inscription is a dedication to an individual named Marcus Salvius Paterno(?), son of Quintus Salvius As[---].

EDCS-10400953.translation: To the divine shades. Iulia Titiola, having died at the age of 23.
EDCS-10400953.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Titiola, who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-10400954.translation: To the divine shades. For Iulia Urit/tior(?).
EDCS-10400954.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the divine shades in memory of Iulia Urit/tior(?).

EDCS-10400955.translation: To the divine shades. For Iuvenis Iulianus, deceased brother, aged 35. He took care to set this up.
EDCS-10400955.summary: This funerary inscription records that someone took care to set up a monument for their deceased brother, Iuvenis Iulianus, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-10400956.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription that begins with the name Marcus L. Florus.

EDCS-10400957.translation: To the divine shades. Laetus, father [---].
EDCS-10400957.summary: This is a funerary inscription fragment beginning with 'To the divine shades' and mentioning Laetus, father.

EDCS-10400958.translation: To the divine shades, to the memory of Laguadius Silvinus, deceased, aged 70. He took care to set this up.
EDCS-10400958.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the memory of Laguadius Silvinus, who died at the age of 70, and was set up by a relative.

EDCS-10400959.translation: To the divine shades and to the memory of Lucretius Fidelis.
EDCS-10400959.summary: This inscription is dedicated to the divine shades and to the memory of Lucretius Fidelis.

EDCS-10400960.translation: To the divine shades. For Lucretia Aurelia, deceased, aged 20.
EDCS-10400960.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucretia Aurelia, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-10400962.summary: This is a funerary inscription fragment commemorating Marcellinus, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-10400963.translation: To the shades of the underworld; of Publius Marcius Hermerotis.
EDCS-10400963.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Publius Marcius Hermerotis.

EDCS-10400965.translation: To the shades of the underworld, for the memory of Maronianus, deceased, aged three years and three months. Secundinus, the father, had this set up for his dearest son.
EDCS-10400965.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a father for his young son, Maronianus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10400966.translation: To the shades of the underworld. For Martinus, or Severus. Crescens, slave of Flavinius, for his brother.
EDCS-10400966.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Crescens, a slave, for his brother Martinus, also known as Severus.

EDCS-10400967.translation: [To the shades of the underworld] and for the memory of Martia Cantusa, deceased, aged 21 years. Her father? had this set up.
EDCS-10400967.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Martia Cantusa, likely set up by her father, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10400968.summary: This fragment records the death of a child named Mascelo, aged three years.
